nodes.title: Keep game state explicit

anchor.snippet: snake, direction, pendingDirection, food, isRunning, isGameOver, score

nodes.why: The board rendering, timer loop, keyboard input, and tests all read from the same predictable component state.
nodes.alternative: Derive game state from DOM classes or mutable element data.
nodes.tradeoff: Explicit state adds fields, but it keeps Angular rendering and unit tests deterministic.

nodes.title: Buffer valid keyboard direction
nodes.why: Keyboard events can happen between timer ticks, so pendingDirection records the next safe move without letting the snake reverse into itself.
nodes.alternative: Update direction immediately for every key press.
nodes.tradeoff: A pending direction is one more piece of state, but it avoids same-tick race conditions and accidental self collisions.

nodes.title: Start exactly one game loop
nodes.why: Movement should advance only on scheduled ticks while the game is running and not over.
nodes.alternative: Move immediately on start or create a new interval every time Start is clicked.
nodes.tradeoff: Guarding and clearing the timer is a little ceremony, but it prevents duplicate loops and speed drift.

nodes.title: Reset from canonical constants
nodes.why: Reset must restore the same initial snake, direction, food, score, running flag, and game-over flag every time.
nodes.alternative: Try to undo the latest moves from the current snake state.
nodes.tradeoff: Constants make reset deterministic, but changing the initial scenario requires updating those constants intentionally.

nodes.title: Check collisions before mutating snake
nodes.why: The next head position decides whether the game ends before the component changes snake body or score state.
nodes.alternative: Move first, then inspect whether the new snake is invalid.
nodes.tradeoff: Pre-checks make the branches more explicit and keep the visible board frozen on the collision frame.

nodes.title: Grow on food, otherwise drop tail
nodes.why: Snake movement is a head prepend plus a tail drop; eating food keeps the tail and increments score.
nodes.alternative: Mutate the existing snake array in place through several pushes and shifts.
nodes.tradeoff: Building nextSnake is easy to reason about for tests, though it allocates a small array each tick.

nodes.title: Choose food from free cells
nodes.why: Food should never appear inside the snake body after a growth tick.
nodes.alternative: Pick random coordinates until one is not occupied.
nodes.tradeoff: Building a free-cell list is straightforward and bounded on a 14x14 board, while retry loops need extra safeguards.

nodes.title: Clear timer on component destroy
nodes.why: Angular can remove the component while an interval is still scheduled, so cleanup prevents state updates after teardown.
nodes.alternative: Rely on the browser to discard timers eventually.
nodes.tradeoff: Lifecycle cleanup is an extra method, but it protects navigation and tests from leaked intervals.
